meta.descriptionTemplate: How coconut shisha charcoal is tested: {{ashMethod}} ash, {{calorificMethod}} calorific value, oven-dry moisture, plus a third-party lab report on every container.

h1: How Coconut Shisha Charcoal Is Tested
heroSubtitle: Every specification is only as good as the method behind it. This page describes how {{brand}} measures each laboratory parameter, which indicators are observed rather than measured, and what an independent laboratory checks on every container.

hero.ctaQuoteAria: Request a test report or COA by WhatsApp
hero.ctaContactAria: Open the inquiry form to request test reports

intro.p1cont: pillar. It owns the laboratory procedure behind each figure; what each parameter means and what a good value is is covered on the specifications-explained page, linked at each method.

keyFacts.heading: Testing at a glance
keyFacts.definition: Which parameters are tested, by whom, to what methods, and what report ships with the order.
keyFacts.parametersValue: Ash, fixed carbon, moisture, volatile matter, calorific value (lab); burn time and ash color (observed)
keyFacts.inHouseValue: Yes — quality control on every batch
keyFacts.reportsValue: Certificate of Analysis + third-party laboratory report

groupA.heading: Laboratory test methods (Tier A)
groupA.intro: Each parameter below is produced by a defined laboratory procedure. Where {{brand}} tests to a recognized standard, it is named; where a procedure is described generically, no specific standard is asserted.

groupB.heading: Observed performance indicators (Tier B)
groupB.intro: These are read under defined conditions, not measured to a laboratory standard. They are honest performance indicators — never assigned an ISO or ASTM method.

ash.body: Ash content is measured by combusting a weighed sample in a furnace until only non-combustible mineral residue remains, then weighing that residue and expressing it as a percentage of the original sample mass.
ash.meaningLinkLabel: what ash content means for your session

fixedCarbon.body: Fixed carbon is determined by proximate analysis: moisture, volatile matter, and ash are each measured, and fixed carbon is calculated as the remainder by difference. It is not weighed directly — it is what is left once the other three are accounted for.
fixedCarbon.meaningLinkLabel: what fixed carbon means for heat output

moisture.body: Moisture is measured by the oven-dry method: a weighed sample is dried at a controlled temperature until its mass stops falling, and the mass lost to evaporation is reported as the moisture content.
moisture.equipment: Drying oven or moisture analyzer and analytical balance
moisture.meaningLinkLabel: what moisture means for lighting and shipping

volatileMatter.body: Volatile matter is measured by heating a sample in a covered crucible without air for a set time, so the gases driven off burn away; the mass lost (after subtracting moisture) is the volatile matter. It is the volatile-matter step of the same proximate analysis that yields fixed carbon.
volatileMatter.equipment: Furnace, covered crucible, and analytical balance
volatileMatter.meaningLinkLabel: what volatile matter means for smoke and odor

calorific.body: Calorific value is determined in a bomb calorimeter: a weighed sample is fully combusted in oxygen inside a sealed vessel, and the heat released is measured from the temperature rise of the surrounding water, then expressed per kilogram.
calorific.meaningLinkLabel: what calorific value means for heat

burnTime.body: Burn time is observed under defined session conditions — a set cube size, a consistent heat source, and a standard bowl setup — and timed until the coal no longer delivers usable heat. It is a performance indicator, not a standardized laboratory measurement, and is never assigned an ISO or ASTM method.
burnTime.meaningLinkLabel: what burn time means in practice

ashColor.body: Ash color is assessed visually after a coal burns out, against consistent lighting. A light, uniform ash is read as a sign of low mineral content and complete combustion. Like burn time, it is an observed indicator, not a laboratory measurement.

independent.heading: Independent and third-party testing
independent.body1: Beyond in-house quality control on every batch, {{brand}} commissions independent verification. A third-party laboratory — {{thirdPartyLabsOr}} — tests {{thirdPartyScope}} on {{thirdPartyFrequency}}, and that report travels with the cargo so the buyer can confirm the specifications against an outside source.
independent.body2: Separately, the Self-Heating Test (SHT) is a test the charcoal undergoes at an accredited laboratory to characterize its self-heating behavior. {{brand}} provides the SHT report as a shipping deliverable.
independent.shtCertIntro: The SHT report as a deliverable is covered on
independent.shtCertLinkLabel: the certifications and test reports page
independent.shtDgIntro: and how the classification it supports works is covered under

video.heading: Burn and ash test
video.body: A short demonstration of a {{brand}} coal through a full burn — lighting, the working session, and the silver-white, low-residue ash it leaves. Recorded under the defined conditions described above.

items.a: Ash content is measured by combusting a weighed sample of charcoal in a furnace until only mineral residue remains, then weighing that residue as a percentage of the starting mass. {{brand}} tests ash to {{ashMethod}}.

Burn time is observed, not measured to a laboratory standard. A coal of a set size is lit under consistent conditions and timed until it no longer delivers usable heat. Because it depends on cube size, heat management, and bowl setup, it is reported as a performance indicator rather than a fixed value.

Calorific value is determined in a bomb calorimeter: a weighed sample is fully burned in oxygen inside a sealed vessel and the heat released is measured from the temperature rise of the surrounding water. {{brand}} tests calorific value to {{calorificMethod}}.

items.q: What standards do you test to?
items.a: {{brand}} tests ash content to {{ashMethod}}, calorific value to {{calorificMethod}}, and fixed carbon and volatile matter by proximate analysis ({{proximateMethod}}); moisture is determined by the oven-dry method. Burn time and ash color are observed performance indicators and are not assigned a laboratory standard.

items.label: Request a sample to test yourself
